texts.full_text: ereal products, ice cream and dairy products. In order to stabilise and preserve fruits, the fruits, or pieces of fruits, are subjected to partial dehydration. However, fruit which is dehydrated sufficiently to prevent spoiling is generally hard with a tough fibrous texture and does not readily reconstitute to a soft acceptable texture. Several techniques are known to obtain partially dried fruits having enhanced softness properties. One such technique is the infusion of partially dried fruits by immersion in a sugar syrup such as a glucose syrup, fructose syrup or corn syrup. In a further development of the above technique dried fruits may be infused with an aqueous solution containing sugar together with an edible polyhydric alcohol such as glycerol, sorbitol or mannitol, as describe ...sorbitol. The addition of these polyhydric alcohols to the sugar syrup helps to improve the texture and softness of the dried fruits, while also enhancing their appearance and shelf life. The use of these ingredients allows for the creation of partially dried fruits that are not only more palatable but also ea

texts.full_text: Treatment options can vary widelydepending on several factors including age and overall health. For splenic marginal Zone B-Cell Lymphoma (SMzl), a common treatment is the removal of part, if not all parts) through an operation called 'splenectomy'. This surgery involves removing your spleens to eliminate cancer cells from that organ. In cases where complete surgical excision isn't possible or preferred due possibly for patient's comorbidities such ascardiovascular issues. Chemotherapy and radiotherapy may be considered, although these treatments are generally less effective in primary splenic lymphoma compared with other types of B-cell non-Hodgkin Lymphomas (NHL). The prognosis is typically favorable especially if the disease has not spread beyond spleen at diagnosis knownas 'localized'. With appropriate treatment and regular follow-ups, many patients can have a long-term survival. However this depends on several factors including patient's overall health status; extent of splenic involvement by lymphoma cells (how much cancer is present in your organ); how well the disease responds to initial therapy. texts.preview_tail: oles that are ofteN overshadowed in the grand, heroic, and often whitewashed, narratives that dominate the american Revolution’s historiography and popular memory. Each group in the class focused on a
texts.full_text: American Revolution By Robert Olwell Last spring, I divided the students enrolled in my course on the “Era of the American Revolution” into groups of four and assigned each group the task of researching, writing, and then producing a four-five minute “video essay.” (For more on the video essay form see “Show & Tell: The Video Essay as History Assignment.”) I called the project “A Revolting People” playing off a line from the Marx Brothers’ film “Duck Soup.” Someone tells Groucho that the “peasants are revolting!” and he replies, “You bet they are, and that’s the problem!” In the project, the “peasants” are the lesser-known, and often overlooked, figures and groups that played significant, and often overlooked, roles in the American Revolution, roles that are ofteN overshadowed in the grand, heroic, and often whitewashed, narratives that dominate the american Revolution’s historiography and popular memory. Each group in the class focused on a

Woman, 28, Fatally Shot in Precinct 6/Towson Baltimore County, Maryland (August 13, 2013) - Police have identified the 28-year-old victim of this morning's homicide in Precinct 6/Towson as Victoria Vernetta Glover of the 1300 Block of Dalton Road. Just before 9:00 a.m. today, Baltimore County Police responded to the 1300 Block of Dalton Road in the Parkville area of Precinct 6 for a report of a shooting. When officers arrived on the scene, they found an adult female suffering from at least one gunshot wound. The female was declared deceased at the scene.
